hero.statsText: Process accuracy through my integrated system control.

modal.headline: Robotic Gastronomy – a new operating system for gastronomy.
modal.intro1: Gastronomy is facing a structural transformation. Over 60,000 open positions, rising labor costs, and growing margin pressure make it clear: the existing operating model is reaching its limits.
modal.intro2: Robotic Gastronomy is the answer. Not as a single technology, but as an integrated operating system built on four pillars:

pillars.title: Automation
pillars.text: Robotics takes over recurring processes in the kitchen, logistics, and service. The result: consistent quality, less dependency on staff, predictable workflows.

pillars.title: Digitalization
pillars.text: Fully networked systems connect ordering, production, and guest retention. Every process becomes measurable and controllable.

pillars.title: AI Integration
pillars.text: Artificial intelligence enables forecasts for revenue, cost of goods, and expenses. Planning replaces gut feeling.

pillars.title: Scalability
pillars.text: Together, these disciplines make gastronomy reproducible – across locations, without complexity and costs growing proportionally.

modal.outro1: Cooking robotics is already being used today by companies such as REWE, Mercedes-Benz, and the German armed forces. The market is growing at around 20% annually. The technology is available – what has been missing so far are business models that integrate it economically.
modal.outro2: This is exactly where Robotic Gastronomy comes in: technology not as an end in itself, but as the foundation for gastronomy that works economically – for operators, investors, and guests.

comparison.mainTitle: Demonstrating the difference between mere hardware purchase and systemic integration.
